text:
Once upon an October night. I flew over sleepy Moore Mooches. There I found a book owl. I took silent circles. It liked my thoughtfulness. I fed him owl owl pellets. Owl eyes glittered. We chatted.

When do I sleep? maybe owls do dreams? Think so.

One orange feather. One green. I kept them. We flew together. No worry birds around.

Sometimes I wish I could snuggle. Night owl's snuggle.

Then I'd have a owl mum. Oh! That's nice. Cute owl thoughts.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
